topics.name: 100 Level: Principles of Accounting & Introduction to Business (UNEC)
topics.description: ACC 101 Principles of Accounting I, BUS 101 Introduction to Business, ECO 101 Economics I.

subtopics.name: ACC 101: Bookkeeping and Financial Statements at UNEC Enugu
objectives: Apply the accounting equation and double-entry system to record business transactions
Prepare final accounts for sole traders and partnerships including adjustments for depreciation and accruals

subtopics.name: ECO 101: Microeconomic Principles for Accounting Students
objectives: Explain demand, supply, and equilibrium price in product and factor markets
Describe business cost structures and profit maximisation relevant to managerial accounting

topics.name: 200–300 Level: Intermediate Accounting, Cost Accounting & Nigerian Tax
topics.description: ACC 201 Intermediate Accounting, ACC 211 Cost Accounting, ACC 301 Nigerian Taxation.

subtopics.name: ACC 201 & ACC 211: Intermediate Financial and Cost Accounting
objectives: Prepare company accounts including cash flow statements under IAS 7 and profit or loss per IAS 1
Calculate job costs, process costs, and apply standard costing and variance analysis techniques

subtopics.name: ACC 301: Nigerian Tax Law and Practice
objectives: Compute personal income tax, companies income tax, petroleum profits tax, and capital gains tax
Understand tax planning strategies and compliance obligations under FIRS regulations

topics.name: 400 Level: Advanced Financial Accounting, Auditing & Research (UNEC)
topics.description: ACC 401 Advanced Financial Reporting, ACC 411 Auditing, ACC 499 Research Project.

subtopics.name: ACC 401 & ACC 411: IFRS Reporting and Auditing Standards
objectives: Apply IFRS standards (IFRS 15, 16, IAS 36, 37) to prepare complex financial statements
Design audit programmes, test internal controls, and issue audit opinions under ISAs adopted by FRC Nigeria

subtopics.name: ACC 499: UNN (UNEC) Accounting Research Dissertation
objectives: Identify and investigate an original accounting or finance problem using quantitative or qualitative research methods
Submit and defend a full research dissertation before the UNN (Enugu Campus) accounting faculty
